video:video.video:title: Spain's Sanchez accuses Israel and Netanyahu of 'illegal abduction' of Gaza flotilla activists
video:video.video:description: Israeli authorities said they were taking two high-profile activists who led an aid flotilla bound for Gaza, and who were captured by Israel in international waters of the Mediterranean Sea, to Israel for questioning.

video:video.video:title: Cubans struggle to survive on pocket-sized government ration book as products dwindle
video:video.video:description: Cuba's pocket-sized government ration book has been circulating for more than six decades. It used to offer a bounty of products, but that offer has dwindled in recent years as the island's economic crisis deepened.

video:video.video:title: Imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi's husband voices family anguish over health fears
video:video.video:description: The health of imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi was at “very high risk," her foundation and family said Saturday, adding that Iran's Intelligence Ministry was opposing her transfer to Tehran for treatment by her own doctors.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will reduce number of troops in Germany 'a lot further' than withdrawal of 5,000
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday the U.S. will reduce number of troops in Germany 'a lot further' than withdrawal of 5,000, escalating a dispute with Chancellor Friedrich Merz as he seeks to scale back America’s commitment to European security.

video:video.video:title: Timmy the humpback whale released in the North Sea after being stranded off Germany for weeks
video:video.video:description: Nicknamed Timmy by German media, the whale was spotted swimming near Germany’s Baltic Sea coast on March 3, far from its natural habitat in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Taiwanese grandmothers aged 89 and 91 train at the gym to stay healthy
video:video.video:description: Taiwan has become a super-aged society with a fifth of its people over the age of 65, but seniors in Taipei City are proving that getting older is not a barrier to health.

video:video.video:title: Spirit Airlines goes out of business after 34 years, ending operations immediately
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines, an impish upstart that shook the industry with its irreverent ads and deep discount fares, announced Saturday that it has gone out of business after 34 years.

video:video.video:title: Most active volcano in the Philippines spews giant ash column sky high
video:video.video:description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said the alert level remained at 3, based on its latest 24-hour monitoring.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he is reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he was reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Hitting the high notes: how Switzerland keeps its yodelling tradition alive
video:video.video:description: Yodelling is alive and well in the Swiss mountains. Swiss yodelling takes two distinct forms - natural yodelling, and yodel songs.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo marks World Press Freedom Day remembering members of media killed at war
video:video.video:description: The United Nations General Assembly proclaimed May 3 as World Press Freedom Day in 1993. 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists worldwide ever recorded, according to The Committee to Protect Journalists.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian father-to-be killed during Israeli military raid as West Bank violence continues
video:video.video:description: Nayef Firas Ziad Samaro, 26, was killed, according to the Health Ministry, and his body was brought to the hospital where his wife was giving birth.

video:video.video:title: Daily life for displaced in Gaza Strip still defined by scarcity, exposure and exhaustion
video:video.video:description: Across makeshift camps, daily life is defined by scarcity, exposure and exhaustion. Tents are torn and unfit for any season, offering little protection from winter rain or the harsh summer heat.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 3: UK reports cargo ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz; Ukraine hits Russian oil port and tankers; Migrant boat runs aground in France, 2 dead; Shakira draws 2M to free Rio beach concert.

video:video.video:title: Planned Parenthood: Goal of mail-order abortion pill ban is akin to a 'national abortion ban'
video:video.video:description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug in what was the biggest jolt to abortion policy since the overturning of Roe v.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Traditional welcome for Taiwan president as he visits Eswatini king
video:video.video:description: Taiwan president Lai Ching-te is welcomed by Eswatini King Mswati III with a military ceremony at Lozitha Palace where they take part in a signing ceremony, bolstering ties with one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies despite growing international pressure from China.

video:video.video:title: All-Indigenous soccer team debuts with victory in Brazil
video:video.video:description: An all-Indigenous soccer team made its professional debut on Sunday in Rio de Janeiro, securing a victory in its first match. AP video by Mario Lobao, Diarlei Rodrigues)

video:video.video:title: Cuba president condemns additional sanctions imposed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has rejected the latest sanctions imposed by the U.S. government against the island — and against anyone who dares to trade with it — and questioned how long the world will continue to tolerate what he considered to be an abusive collective punishment by the U.S. ad

video:video.video:title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, his spokesman says
video:video.video:description: Rudolph Giuliani, the ex-mayor of New York City and a former adviser to President Donald Trump, is in critical but stable condition at a hospital, his spokesman said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Laid off Spirit flight attendant reacts to news of airline shutting down
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines announced it is going out of business after 34 years, saying Saturday it has started winding down operations.

video:video.video:title: Beer, brass and 'Hau-Ruck' shouts as a Bavarian village raises its new maypole
video:video.video:description: It's May, and in the Bavarian village of Kühbach, residents are marking the first day of the month by raising a single decorated tree trunk known as a maypole, a custom observed across southern Germany, Austria and parts of central Europe.

video:video.video:title: Riders put hobby horses through their paces at Germany's second championship
video:video.video:description: It was giddy up and go in Frankfurt on Saturday as the country's hobby horsers took part in their second ever championships.

video:video.video:title: Alex Zanardi, Italian auto racing champion who turned Paralympic gold medalist, dies at 59
video:video.video:description: Paying tribute to Zanardi, Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni said on X that Italy had lost "a great champion and an extraordinary man".

video:video.video:title: New York City police release footage of machete-wielding man shot at Grand Central
video:video.video:description: New York City police have released body-worn camera footage of officers shooting and killing a machete-wielding man who stabbed three people at a Grand Central subway stop last month.

video:video.video:title: Severe weather leaves shipwrecks strewn across the Queensland coast
video:video.video:description: Dozens of vessels lie wrecked along the coastline of northern Australia after a season of severe summer weather, leaving state authorities to handle the cleanup.

video:video.video:title: 2 killed after Russian drone attack in Ukraine's southern Odesa region
video:video.video:description: The drones also hit port infrastructure, causing a fire that was later extinguished by emergency teams, the emergency service reported.

video:video.video:title: Landlords seek relief from unpaid rent in lawsuit against federal eviction ban
video:video.video:description: A group of landlords are hoping to settle with the federal government over what they say are billions of dollars in losses due to the federal eviction moratorium in place for part of the pandemic.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Milly Alcock’s 'punk rock' Supergirl takes flight
video:video.video:description: Milly Alcock says she sees a lot of herself in Supergirl, and jokes about how surreal it is to have her own action figure. (May 2)

video:video.video:title: Leaders gather in Yerevan for European Political Community meeting as Ukraine and Iran wars loom
video:video.video:description: The summit will be co-chaired by European Council President António Costa and Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan.

video:video.video:title: The founding fathers didn’t create a Christian nation but religion did shape their thinking
video:video.video:description: The long-running debate over the founders’ intentions about religion has been turbocharged with the nation's approaching 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: How a bird protection group bought a forest rich in biodiversity
video:video.video:description: A rare pocket of woodland in northeastern Serbia has been saved from being cut down. A bird protection society raised 8,000 euros ($9,500) in a crowdfunding campaign to buy Nightingale's Forest and protect the flora and fauna that live there.

video:video.video:title: Shakira's huge free concert on Copacabana Beach draws 2 million fans
video:video.video:description: The performance followed similar shows by Madonna in 2024 and Lady Gaga last year — also attended by huge crowds that danced on the sprawling sands.

video:video.video:title: For foreign workers in the Middle East, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
video:video.video:description: For 15 years, Mohammad Abdullah Al Mamun worked in Saudi Arabia, sending money home to his family in one of the poorest areas of Bangladesh.

video:video.video:title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City injures at least 10
video:video.video:description: Police in an Oklahoma City suburb say that at least 10 people have been taken to hospitals following a shooting at a party at a lake.
